title: Dactylorchis sambucina
taxonomy: Dactylorchis sambucina is a species of orchid that is now classified as Coeloglossum viride. This classification is due to a reevaluation of the genus Dactylorchis. The reclassification has been adopted in various botanical sources. The species is still commonly referred to as Dactylorchis sambucina in some contexts. The reclassification reflects changes in our understanding of orchid taxonomy.
uses: Dactylorchis sambucina, also known as the summer-spurred orchid, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It has been employed to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant's root and leaves are often used in herbal remedies, and it is believed to have antioxidant effects. In some cultures, Dactylorchis sambucina is used to treat digestive issues and to reduce fever. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
benefits: Dactylorchis sambucina, also known as the Sambur orchid, has been studied for its potential health benefits. The plant contains compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. These properties could contribute to the plant's potential use in treating various health conditions, including those related to cardiovascular and neurodegenerative diseases. Additionally, Dactylorchis sambucina has been used in traditional medicine to aid in digestion and relieve respiratory issues. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's effectiveness and potential applications in human health.
properties: Dactylorchis sambucina, also known as the Samburu orchid, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's extracts may have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which could contribute to its potential use in wound healing and skin care. Research suggests that Dactylorchis sambucina may also exhibit anti-cancer properties, although more studies are needed to confirm this. constituents: Dactylorchis sambucina contains iridoids and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant's iridoids, such as sambunigron, are responsible for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Sambunigron has been shown to exhibit strong antioxidant activity and can help protect against cell damage. The phenolic compounds present in Dactylorchis sambucina may also contribute to its antiviral and antifungal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of this plant.
parts: Dactylorchis sambucina, also known as the bog orchid, is a type of orchid that has been used in traditional medicine. The medicinal parts of Dactylorchis sambucina are not well-documented, but it is believed that the plant's roots, leaves, and flowers may be used for their medicinal properties. side_effects: Dactylorchis sambucina, also known as the lesser helmet orchid, is a rare and endangered species found in Europe.
